plus.analyst_notes:
Closes https://github.com/vz-risk/VCDB/issues/23386
Encoded by AI — encode-veris-incident skill version 20260618T195537Z

Open Arms Care Corporation (OAC), a Brentwood, TN nonprofit providing residential/therapeutic care for individuals with disabilities (NAICS 623210), suffered an email-account compromise. An unauthorized third party accessed an OAC email account/tenant between June and August 2025; the activity was detected internally in August 2025 (sources say "unusual/suspicious email activity"). Forensic review completed April 30, 2026; notification letters mailed June 9, 2026.

Coding decisions / assumptions:
- Actor: coded external per the issue's `Hacking-ext-unknown` template tag. No named threat actor in any source -> actor.external.variety = Unknown, country Unknown. Motive inferred Financial (theft of SSNs, PHI, and financial-account data from a mailbox is the classic financial-motive email compromise).
- Action: entry method/vector undisclosed (`hacking-ext-unknown` template) -> action.hacking.variety = Unknown, vector = Unknown. result = Infiltrate only; data acquisition/exfiltration was not confirmed (notice says emails "may have been accessed or acquired"), so Exfiltrate was deliberately NOT coded.
- Asset: the compromise was of an email account within OAC's "email tenant" -> S - Mail; "tenant" language implies a hosted/cloud mail service, so cloud = External Cloud Asset(s). Sources state the incident was limited to information transmitted via email and did not involve other OAC systems.
- Attribute: issue is tagged `Breach` and the actor had direct access to a mailbox containing names + SSN + medical diagnosis/treatment + health-insurance info, so data_disclosure = Yes (unauthorized access to PHI = confidentiality compromise). NOTE: the org's notices use hedged "may have been accessed or acquired" language and did not confirm exfiltration. confidentiality.state defaulted to Stored (mailbox at rest; encryption status not stated). data_abuse = Unknown (no downstream misuse reported as of notification).
- Data varieties: Personal (names, SSN), Medical (diagnosis, treatment, health-insurance info), Bank (financial account info, involved in "a small number of instances").
- data_total omitted: number of affected individuals not publicly disclosed.
- Discovery: detected internally (OAC identified suspicious email activity) -> discovery_method.internal; specific detection mechanism not stated -> variety Unknown.
- Timeline: incident began June 2025 (initial access), discovered Aug 2025 -> discovery duration ~2 months. plus.dbir_year = 2025 (begin year), not the 2026 notification year and not the `PHIDBR2019` label (ignored as an artifact tag).
- victim.employee_count = '101 to 1000' per reviewer assessment (PR #23389 feedback from swidup); OAC is a 35+ year nonprofit serving hundreds of clients in TN.

Sources consulted: HIPAA Journal article and OAC's official PR Newswire data-security-incident notice (see reference field).

REVIEW UPDATE (post swidup PR #23389 feedback): set employee_count to '101 to 1000' (was previously left 'Unknown').

event_chain.summary: Unauthorized access to OAC email account/tenant (method undisclosed)
Emails containing PII/PHI accessible to the intruder

summary: Open Arms Care Corporation, a Brentwood, Tennessee nonprofit providing residential and therapeutic care for individuals with disabilities, discovered in August 2025 that an unauthorized third party had accessed one of its email accounts between June and August 2025. Emails in the account contained individuals' names along with Social Security numbers, medical diagnosis and treatment information, health insurance information, and in a small number of cases financial account information. The investigation completed April 30, 2026 and notifications were mailed June 9, 2026. No threat actor was identified and the number of affected individuals was not disclosed.
